mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods of HOUST growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 1% signals aggressive monetary easing, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS < 1% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate supply-side shocks and stagflation risks, which predict negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods of INDPRO decline and CPI rise are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) combined with high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal reduced corporate profitability, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS rise and CPI high are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) indicate robust labor market conditions, which correlate with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to sustained consumer spending and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns in periods of UNRATE > 5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicate weakening demand, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to reduced corporate revenues and earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods of RSAFS and GDPC1 decline are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction's Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply shocks, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to stagflationary pressures.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns in periods of INDPRO drop and CPI rise are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction's Rank IC < 0.03.
